questions.question: Which word is a noun in the sentence "The artist painted a bright mural"?
Which word is a verb in the sentence "Birds fly above the trees"?
Which word is an adjective in the sentence "She wore a colorful scarf"?
Which word is an adverb in the sentence "The puppy barked loudly at night"?
Which word is a pronoun in the sentence "They finished the project early"?
Which word is a preposition in the sentence "The keys are under the table"?
Which word is a conjunction in the sentence "Mia and Leo studied together"?
Which word is an interjection in the sentence "Wow! That trick was amazing"?
In the sentence "The river flows calmly", what part of speech is the word "river"?
In the sentence "Carlos quickly opened the window", what part of speech is the word "quickly"?
Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.
choices: The dogs runs in the yard.
The dogs run in the yard.
The dogs running in the yard.
The dogs is running in the yard.
questions.answer: The dogs run in the yard.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb: "My brother ____ soccer after school."
Choose the correct verb: "The students ____ ready for the test."
Which sentence is correct?
choices: Each of the players have a uniform.
Each of the players has a uniform.
Each of the players are wearing a uniform.
Each of the players need a uniform.
questions.answer: Each of the players has a uniform.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb: "Neither the cat nor the dogs ____ outside."
Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.
choices: There is many books on the shelf.
There are many books on the shelf.
There was many books on the shelf.
There be many books on the shelf.
questions.answer: There are many books on the shelf.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb: "A basket of apples ____ on the table."
Which sentence is correct?
choices: The team are winning the game.
The team is winning the game.
The team have winning the game.
questions.answer: The team is winning the game.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb: "One of the cookies ____ missing."
Choose the correct verb: "The teacher and the principal ____ meeting today."
In the sentence "The desert was arid, with almost no rain for months", what does "arid" mean?
In the sentence "Lena felt gloomy after the picnic was canceled, but she cheered up later", what does "gloomy" mean?
In the sentence "The kitten was timid and hid behind the couch when guests arrived", what does "timid" mean?
In the sentence "The coach said our victory was possible because the other team looked fatigued and slow", what does "fatigued" mean?
In the sentence "Mina gave a brief answer, just two words long", what does "brief" mean?
In the sentence "The ancient vase was fragile, so everyone handled it with care", what does "fragile" mean?
In the sentence "The children were eager to open their gifts and could hardly wait", what does "eager" mean?
In the sentence "After walking for miles, we were relieved to find a tiny cabin that offered shelter", what does "shelter" mean?
In the sentence "Jared's explanation was vague, so no one understood exactly what he meant", what does "vague" mean?
In the sentence "The path was narrow, so only one person could walk on it at a time", what does "narrow" mean?

questions.passage: Maya planted tomato seeds in a small garden behind her house. Every morning, she watered the soil and checked for sprouts. After two weeks, tiny green stems appeared. Maya smiled because her hard work was paying off.
questions.question: Why did Maya smile at the end of the passage?
choices: She found a new garden tool.
She sold tomatoes at the market.

questions.passage: Maya planted tomato seeds in a small garden behind her house. Every morning, she watered the soil and checked for sprouts. After two weeks, tiny green stems appeared. Maya smiled because her hard work was paying off.
questions.question: What did Maya do every morning?
choices: Watered the soil and checked for sprouts
questions.answer: Watered the soil and checked for sprouts

questions.passage: During lunch, Evan noticed that his friend Sam was unusually quiet. Sam usually laughed and told jokes, but today he just stared at his sandwich. Evan asked if something was wrong. Sam explained that he had forgotten about a big science project due that afternoon. Evan offered to help him organize his materials before class.
questions.question: Why was Sam unusually quiet?
choices: He was upset about a science project.
questions.answer: He was upset about a science project.

questions.passage: During lunch, Evan noticed that his friend Sam was unusually quiet. Sam usually laughed and told jokes, but today he just stared at his sandwich. Evan asked if something was wrong. Sam explained that he had forgotten about a big science project due that afternoon. Evan offered to help him organize his materials before class.
questions.question: What does Evan's action show about him?

questions.passage: The school library announced a weekend reading challenge. Students who read for at least three hours would receive a bookmark and have their names posted on the bulletin board. Priya loved mysteries, so she borrowed two novels on Friday. By Sunday evening, she had finished both books and written short notes about her favorite characters.
questions.question: Why did Priya borrow two novels?
choices: She needed them for science class.
She wanted to take part in the reading challenge.
She was told to replace lost books.
questions.answer: She wanted to take part in the reading challenge.

questions.passage: The school library announced a weekend reading challenge. Students who read for at least three hours would receive a bookmark and have their names posted on the bulletin board. Priya loved mysteries, so she borrowed two novels on Friday. By Sunday evening, she had finished both books and written short notes about her favorite characters.
questions.question: Which detail best shows that Priya enjoyed the books?
choices: She wrote notes about her favorite characters.
She returned the books on Monday.
questions.answer: She wrote notes about her favorite characters.

questions.passage: Mr. Lee's class built paper bridges during science period. The goal was to create a bridge strong enough to hold a stack of coins. Some bridges bent right away, but Sofia's design stayed firm. Her group had folded the paper into triangles, which made the bridge stronger.
questions.question: What was the goal of the activity?
choices: To create a bridge that could hold coins
questions.answer: To create a bridge that could hold coins

questions.passage: Mr. Lee's class built paper bridges during science period. The goal was to create a bridge strong enough to hold a stack of coins. Some bridges bent right away, but Sofia's design stayed firm. Her group had folded the paper into triangles, which made the bridge stronger.
questions.question: Why was Sofia's bridge successful?
choices: She used metal instead of paper.
She taped coins to the desk.
Her group folded the paper into triangles.
She made the bridge very short.
questions.answer: Her group folded the paper into triangles.

questions.passage: On Saturday morning, Luis planned to ride his bike to the park. Dark clouds gathered, and soon rain began to fall. Instead of feeling disappointed, Luis brought out his paints and spent the afternoon creating a picture of the storm from his window.
questions.question: Why did Luis change his plans?
choices: The park was closed for repairs.

questions.passage: On Saturday morning, Luis planned to ride his bike to the park. Dark clouds gathered, and soon rain began to fall. Instead of feeling disappointed, Luis brought out his paints and spent the afternoon creating a picture of the storm from his window.
questions.question: What can the reader infer about Luis?
choices: He knows how to adapt when plans change.
questions.answer: He knows how to adapt when plans change.

questions.passage: Nora's class was collecting canned food for a community pantry. At first, the box near the classroom door was nearly empty. Nora made colorful posters and asked the principal if she could speak during morning announcements. By the end of the week, the box was overflowing, and the class had to bring in a second one.
questions.question: What problem did Nora notice at first?
choices: Not many canned foods had been donated.
The class lost the collection box.
questions.answer: Not many canned foods had been donated.

questions.passage: Nora's class was collecting canned food for a community pantry. At first, the box near the classroom door was nearly empty. Nora made colorful posters and asked the principal if she could speak during morning announcements. By the end of the week, the box was overflowing, and the class had to bring in a second one.
questions.question: Which action helped increase donations?
choices: Nora hid the box in the classroom.
Nora made posters and spoke during announcements.
questions.answer: Nora made posters and spoke during announcements.

questions.passage: Tariq had never baked bread before, but he wanted to surprise his grandmother. He carefully measured the flour, yeast, and water. When the dough did not rise, he reread the recipe and realized he had used cold water instead of warm water. On his second try, the dough doubled in size, and the kitchen smelled wonderful.
questions.question: Why did Tariq's first batch of dough fail to rise?

questions.passage: Tariq had never baked bread before, but he wanted to surprise his grandmother. He carefully measured the flour, yeast, and water. When the dough did not rise, he reread the recipe and realized he had used cold water instead of warm water. On his second try, the dough doubled in size, and the kitchen smelled wonderful.
questions.question: What lesson did Tariq likely learn?
choices: Bread cannot be made at home.

questions.passage: For the class talent show, Alina wanted to play the violin. She was nervous because she had only performed in front of her family before. Each evening, she practiced the song slowly and then a little faster. On the night of the show, she took a deep breath, stepped onto the stage, and played without missing a note.
questions.question: How did Alina prepare for the talent show?
choices: She bought a new violin that day.
She asked someone else to perform.

questions.passage: For the class talent show, Alina wanted to play the violin. She was nervous because she had only performed in front of her family before. Each evening, she practiced the song slowly and then a little faster. On the night of the show, she took a deep breath, stepped onto the stage, and played without missing a note.
questions.question: What is the best main idea of the passage?
choices: Alina forgot her song on stage.
Violin is the easiest instrument to learn.

questions.passage: The city park added a butterfly garden near the pond. Volunteers planted flowers that bloom in spring and summer. Soon, families began visiting the garden to watch butterflies rest on the petals. Signs beside the path explained how the plants provide food for caterpillars and butterflies.
questions.question: Why did families visit the butterfly garden?
choices: To go fishing in the pond.
To watch butterflies in the flowers.
questions.answer: To watch butterflies in the flowers.

questions.passage: The city park added a butterfly garden near the pond. Volunteers planted flowers that bloom in spring and summer. Soon, families began visiting the garden to watch butterflies rest on the petals. Signs beside the path explained how the plants provide food for caterpillars and butterflies.
questions.question: What do the signs beside the path explain?
choices: Why the pond is being cleaned
How the plants help caterpillars and butterflies
When the park closes each day
questions.answer: How the plants help caterpillars and butterflies

questions.passage: Zoe and her cousin were building a puzzle on a rainy afternoon. At first, they searched for edge pieces and sorted colors into small piles. The sky section was difficult because many pieces looked alike, but they kept trying. After an hour, they snapped in the last piece and cheered.
questions.question: What challenge did Zoe and her cousin face?
choices: The sky section had many similar pieces.
They did not have enough table space.
The puzzle was missing all the edge pieces.
questions.answer: The sky section had many similar pieces.

questions.passage: Zoe and her cousin were building a puzzle on a rainy afternoon. At first, they searched for edge pieces and sorted colors into small piles. The sky section was difficult because many pieces looked alike, but they kept trying. After an hour, they snapped in the last piece and cheered.
questions.question: Which word best describes Zoe and her cousin?
